purpose: Explains how the current cross-asset pricing inconsistency persists through self-reinforcing loops until a discrete catalyst breaks one link, triggering simultaneous non-linear repricing
components: Kalecki loop: deficit → profits → equity prices → wealth effect → tax revenue → deficit reduction
Basis trade loop: synthetic demand → yield suppression → lower fiscal cost → continued issuance
Covenant-lite loop: masked distress → compressed spreads → continued issuance → more hidden distress
Self-reinforcing stability with discrete break threshold
current_assessment: The equilibrium is currently self-reinforcing through multiple loops. Cross-asset inconsistency (equities 21-22x, IG 90-110bp, term premium 50-100bp) persists because each market prices a different resolution. Resolution pathway disputed: meta-stable simultaneous break vs standard 4-8 month sequential repricing (rates, FX, credit, equity). Neither framework clearly superior.
limitations: Cannot predict the timing or nature of the catalyst
The distinction between meta-stable break and sequential repricing is ex post, not ex ante
Self-reinforcing loops can persist far longer than fundamentals suggest
The framework is not falsifiable until a break actually occurs
